reply_text:
Usagi's decision to go to the Borderlands again was not random. When I rewatched the series again, there were scenes of Usagi grieving her father's death and that still stayed with her throughout the series. She also had a flashback when her father told her to live her life even if he's gonna d*e, that just doesn't sit right for her and to even us, the audience. 

Also, keep in mind that she forgot about her time in the Borderlands already. Then she was manipulated by Ryuji, thinking she's gonna meet her father in Borderlands since he's always in her mind like haunting her (meaning he hasn't passed on to heaven yet or reincarnated whatever you believe). She didn't know Ryuji's full background, or that if she agreed, he would pull her back to the Borderlands. 

So, Usagi's decision was not random at all. She has unresolved issues, she doesn't want to open it up to Arisu since she is living a happy life like what her father wants her to have. But still want to have closure for her father's d*ath.

reply_text: ​@DipankarPanda-l7f Ehhh.... It was never about the physical "saving" humanity rather than metaphorically. What is humanity. These questions were frequently raised through all seasons to which those who support the games were either greedy or understood humans were greedy. The protagonist and select others saw humanity as kindness. The story was terrible... Season 1 yeah it was awesome but the continuation in Season 2 & 3 felt like the messaging they wanted us to take over was lost due to lack of reward of being kind. I actually didn't mind the baby winning in the sole purpose that kindness doesn't need to be given to people we believe deserve it but should be given regardless. Hence why the baby winning was chosen but the journey to there felt like it was stupid to be kind rather than it was best to be kind to save ourselves. We saw suicide, sacrifice for the people to die next game or before the next game. Alice in Borderland felt like it actually got better in season 2, the story in that was changing ourselves for better, regardless if its too late. We saw all the characters reflect in the past and change. The only thing I'll add both series had some bad games, though alice in borderland it was more balanced until the second last game where most of the cast got shot and somehow survived. I would argue Alice was better for the story, Squid Games was better for the games in general.
